title: Agentic Web Navigator — Electron <-> Python WebSocket protocol
description: All messages are JSON objects with a 'type' field and a 'payload' object. The Python agent runs the WebSocket SERVER; the Electron shell is the CLIENT.

hello.description: Sent by Electron once the browser is up and the CDP port is listening.

ready.description: Python confirms it has attached to Chromium over CDP.

task.start.description: User submitted a natural-language task in the agent panel.

agent.status.description: state in: thinking | acting | waiting_human | done | error

agent.action.description: One executed action, streamed to the live action log.

human.help.request.description: Agent is blocked (e.g. unsolvable CAPTCHA). Electron shows the takeover overlay.

human.help.resolved.description: Human finished manual interaction; agent re-observes and resumes.
